cases.prompt: Make the search endpoint fast: p95 under 150ms at 100 RPS, measured with our k6 script. Implement and prove it.
expected_decisions: Quality Targets list recorded with metric, target, and measurement method for the p95 latency requirement
Measured result is filled in the Quality Targets list before the quality gate
expected_evidence: k6 measurement command and its result are recorded at gate time
cases.notes: Non-embedded server latency/throughput target with an explicit measurement method should route through the generic NFR path, not embedded.

cases.prompt: The importer should be fast and scalable. Improve it.
expected_decisions: Vague quality words (fast, scalable) are converted into a Quality Targets list entry with metric, target, and measurement method before implementation proceeds
cases.notes: Vague quality words need measurable targets defined before implementation, without invoking embedded or architecture-comparison workflows.

cases.prompt: The robot-side recorder must stay under 3% CPU on the target device, measured by the on-target harness.
cases.notes: Physical target constraint on an embedded/robot-local recorder routes to the embedded NFR pipeline, not the generic non-embedded performance-review path.
